@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a180
+project: file-edit-tools
+title: files tool module ŌĆö close coverage gaps in edit/shared_utils/write/read (error & cleanup branches)
+---
+## What
+Test-only task: lift the `files/` tool module (the file-edit-tools feature surface) from ~94% toward ~99-100% line coverage. The new crates are already done (`hashline` 98.4% ŌĆö only its `Display` impl; `edit-match` 100%). This card targets the tool-side files we own. Measured with `cargo llvm-cov nextest -p swissarmyhammer-tools --summary-only` and `cargo llvm-cov report --show-missing-lines -p swissarmyhammer-tools`.
+
+Current line coverage (files we own):
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/edit/mod.rs` ŌĆö 94.4% (~104 missed)
+- `ŌĆ”/files/shared_utils.rs` ŌĆö 90.3% (~69 missed)
+- `ŌĆ”/files/write/mod.rs` ŌĆö 94.6% (~31 missed)
+- `ŌĆ”/files/read/mod.rs` ŌĆö 96.4% (~18 missed)
+- `ŌĆ”/files/mod.rs` (dispatch) ŌĆö 96.9% (~16 missed)
+- `ŌĆ”/files/schema.rs` ŌĆö 100% (done)
+
+**Out of scope:** `files/glob/mod.rs` (89.9%) and `files/grep/mod.rs` (94.6%) are PRE-EXISTING tools not built/changed by this feature ŌĆö leave them for a separate card; do not pad them here.
+
+The uncovered lines are mostly **error / cleanup / permission branches** that the happy-path tests don't reach, plus a few `Debug`/`Display`/trace lines. They need fault-injection tests (assert real behavior), not line-chasing. Get the exact current line list with `cargo llvm-cov report --show-missing-lines -p swissarmyhammer-tools` (filter to the files above) before starting ŌĆö the snapshot below is a guide.
+
+Known clusters to target (from the latest report):
+- `edit/mod.rs`: 140-153, 287-320, 687-730, 979-992, 1021-1037, 1069-1095, 1144-1191, 1220-1223, 1290-1294 ŌĆö these are the temp-file/rename/permission/encoding **error-cleanup arms** of `commit_content`/`write_with_encoding`/`read_with_encoding_detection` and the validation/idempotency edge branches. Exercise via fault injection: read-only target dir/file, non-UTF-8 content (decode error), an unwritable temp path, a deliberately mismatched-length find/replace remainder, etc.
+- `shared_utils.rs`: 284-306, 721-766, 928-952, 1578-1584 ŌĆö error paths in `SecureFileAccess`/permission checks/`handle_file_error`/`window_lines`/`whole_file_hash` boundaries. Cover with permission-denied, path-traversal-rejected, binary-file, and boundary (empty/last-line) inputs.
+- `write/mod.rs`: 211-215, 237-240, 265-287, 367/390/395 ŌĆö the atomic-write failure/cleanup and guard edge branches (rename failure cleanup, permission failure).
+- `read/mod.rs`: 108-111, 218-222, 305/340/365/392/419/451 ŌĆö offset/limit boundary and error branches.
+- `files/mod.rs`: 76-78, 123, 237-252 ŌĆö the read-only-subset rejection messages and unknown-op/inference error arms.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `cargo llvm-cov nextest -p swissarmyhammer-tools --summary-only` shows each of `edit/mod.rs`, `shared_utils.rs`, `write/mod.rs`, `read/mod.rs`, `files/mod.rs` at Ōēź98% lines (aim 100%; `glob`/`grep` untouched).
+- [ ] New tests assert real BEHAVIOR on the error paths (e.g. a read-only file yields the expected error AND leaves the original byte-identical; a non-UTF-8 file is rejected; an atomic-write failure cleans up its temp file), not just line execution.
+- [ ] Any branch that is genuinely unreachable without contrived fault injection is either (a) covered by a real fault-injection test, or (b) refactored/removed if dead ŌĆö NOT padded with a meaningless test. Document any line deliberately left uncovered and why.
+- [ ] No production behavior change beyond justified dead-code removal; all existing files tests stay green.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Add fault-injection + boundary tests to the relevant `edit/mod.rs`, `write/mod.rs`, `read/mod.rs`, `shared_utils.rs`, `files/mod.rs` test modules (and/or `tests/integration/`). Prefer `#[cfg(unix)]` permission tricks (already used by existing tests like `test_edit_atomic_operation_failure_cleanup`) for the cleanup paths.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools` green (NEVER plain `cargo test`; doctests via `--doc`).
+- [ ] `cargo llvm-cov nextest -p swissarmyhammer-tools --summary-only` confirms the Ōēź98% per-file targets.
+- [ ] `cargo fmt` + `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-tools -- -D warnings` clean.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö for each gap, write the fault-injection test that exercises the error branch, confirm it covers the target lines via `--show-missing-lines`, then move on.
\ No newline at end of file
